title: AI Engineer
company_focus:
Software for landscapers and arborists - estimating,
scheduling, training, payments
description:
What Makes Us Stand Out:

Granum is the leading software company devoted to serving
landscapers and arborists across North America, providing
intuitive, industry-specific solutions designed to help them
improve and grow their businesses while impressing their
customers. Granum brings together three of the most trusted
software names in the industry — LMN, SingleOps, and
Greenius — into one powerful software ecosystem. More than
just a software company, Granum works alongside its clients
to implement systems for estimating, scheduling, crew
training, invoicing, and payments, blending technology with
hands-on onboarding and human support. The company's mission
is to help industry professionals unlock their potential and
achieve their most important goals, all united by the belief
that its clients’ success is not just a milestone, but a
shared mission.

As a team, we’re on a mission to reshape an age-old
industry, and we’re looking for people who thrive on
challenging and meaningful work. Our environment is highly
collaborative, innovative, and supportive, with a shared
commitment to having fun while making an impact. We live by
our core values: We Before Me, Bias to Act, The Extra 1%,
Accountable to Outcomes, Unconditional Transparency, and
Forgiveness. If this resonates with you, we’d love to meet
you and explore how you can help us build the future of the
green industry!

Our culture and growth has been recognized with multiple
awards, it’s a great time to join!

- Comparably 2024 #23/100 Best (small/ medium sized companies)
  Leadership Teams
- Comparably 2024 #63/75 (small/ medium sized companies)
  Happiest Employees
- #9 Atlanta Business Chronicle's 2024 Best Places to Work
  (Our second year in a row!)
- #6 of Built In’s 2024 Best Places to Work: Top 50 start-ups
  in Atlanta  (Our second year in a row!)
- Inc Magazine’s 2023 Best Places to Work
- Inc. 5000: #131 of 2024 Southeast Regional America’s Fastest
  Growing Companies. (Our third year in a row!)
- Inc 5000 America's fastest-growing private companies (Our
  third year in a row!)
- Certified Great Place to Work Canada 2024

The Role

As an AI Engineer, you'll be foundational in creating the
next stage of our product's evolution. At Granum, we measure
engineering success by customer outcomes, not just pull
requests. You'll join the AI Platforms pod, a team uniquely
positioned at the intersection of product innovation and
organizational transformation. Your work will be dual-track:
fueling the evolution of the customer experience through
intelligent features while simultaneously architecting the
frameworks that enable AI-driven capabilities across the
company.

Outcomes You'll Drive

- The Agentic Leap: Design and deploy autonomous AI agents
  that go beyond simple prompts to take action, complete
  tasks, and operate as a workforce multiplier for our
  customers.
- Cross-Pod Enablement: Architect the internal tooling and
  shared patterns that empower every engineering pod to
  seamlessly integrate AI into their specific domains and
  leverage AI-assisted development workflows.
- Intelligent Integration: Implement and optimize robust LLM
  orchestration systems using our proprietary industry data
  to provide the intelligent foundation our product pods
  draft off of.
- Technical Excellence: Contribute to the full AI/ML
  lifecycle, from model evaluation to deployment, ensuring
  our domain knowledge is protected by high-quality,
  production-ready features.

We're looking for...

A pragmatic, product-minded engineer who thrives at the
nexus of real customer problems and fast-paced engineering.

Required Experience:

- 6+ years of software engineering experience with at least
  one year building with AI models.
- Strong Programming Skills: Proficiency in .NET/C# and Python
  within an established codebase.
- Collaborative Enabler: An engineer who excels at translating
  complex AI concepts into actionable patterns for others
  and enjoys mentoring cross-functional peers to elevate the
  entire organization's AI fluency.
- AI Tooling: Hands-on experience with LLMs like GPT or Claude
  and orchestration frameworks such as LangChain or
  LlamaIndex.
- Data Architecture: Experience with vector databases (e.g.,
  Pinecone, Chroma), embedding techniques, and RAG.
- Ownership: Proven ability to take complex AI features from
  ideation to production.

Nice-to-Haves:

- Experience building AI Agents and autonomous agentic
  workflows.
- Familiarity with Snowflake Cortex or LaunchDarkly AI
  Configs.

Compensation:

- Canada-based candidates: CAD 150,000 - 185,000
- US-based candidates: USD 160,000 - 200,000

#LI-Remote

Reasons why you would love it here!

- Join a team culture that’s all about collaboration, support,
  and having fun while making a real impact every day.
- In the U.S., we offer comprehensive medical, dental, and
  vision coverage with multiple plan options, plus
  additional add ons like HSA/FSA accounts, disability and
  life insurance, and more.
- In Canada, we provide an employer-funded HSA-based benefits
  plan with drug, dental, and mental health coverage -
  giving you a flexible way to manage your healthcare needs.
- We're committed to your financial future, with 401(k)
  matching for U.S. employees and RRSP matching for those in
  Canada.
- We invest in your growth through tailored career development
  conversations and support for tools, courses, and
  resources to help you thrive.
- And when it comes to work-life balance? We offer unlimited
  Paid Time Off, paid company holidays, and a company-wide
  winter break from December 24 to January 1 - so you can
  truly recharge.

Granum does not sponsor work authorization needs; candidates
must have proper work authorization to work for any employer
in Canada or the U.S, without sponsorship from the company.

Granum is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative
Action Employer. We consider qualified applicants without
regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation,
gender identity or expression, national origin, age, marital
status, protected veteran status, or disability status.
Granum participates in the federal E-Verify program.

Granum is committed to providing accessible employment
opportunities in accordance with the Accessibility for
Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005. Granum reserves the
right to change job descriptions as per the needs of the
organization. For accommodation requests, please contact
hr@granum.com
responsibilities: Design and deploy autonomous AI agents that complete tasks and act as workforce multipliers for customers
Architect internal tooling and shared patterns to enable AI integration across engineering pods
Implement and optimize LLM orchestration systems using proprietary industry data
Contribute to full AI/ML lifecycle from model evaluation to production deployment
Build production-ready AI features that protect domain knowledge
Mentor cross-functional peers and elevate organizational AI fluency
Enable AI-assisted development workflows across teams
use_cases: Autonomous AI agents for landscaping and arborist business operations
Intelligent features for customer experience improvement
AI-powered estimation, scheduling, and invoicing automation
Domain-specific AI capabilities leveraging proprietary industry data
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role is clearly AI-first as it involves designing and
deploying autonomous AI agents, implementing LLM
orchestration systems, building RAG systems, and working on
the full AI/ML lifecycle from evaluation to deployment. The
engineer will directly build and productionize AI
capabilities.
